title: Computer Science Engineer Intern | Entry Level | Fresher | Software / Systems / Remote
description:

 This role is ideal for aspiring professionals who want to build careers in software engineering, technical systems, digital infrastructure, or computational problem\\-solving.

 As a Computer Science Engineer Intern, you will support practical assignments involving software systems, algorithms, technical workflows, engineering concepts, and implementation\\-focused project tasks. You will gain real\\-world exposure to core technical environments while strengthening analytical capability and professional execution.

description:

title: Embedded Software Engineer(Freelance | WFH)
description:
**About BeGig** 




 BeGig is a tech freelancing platform that connects skilled technology professionals with companies and founders seeking specialized expertise on a flexible, project\\-based basis.
 



  





**Your Opportunity** 




 As an Embedded Software Engineer on BeGig, you’ll work on freelance projects focused on developing software for embedded systems, IoT devices, and hardware\\-driven products. You’ll help organizations build reliable, efficient, and high\\-performance software that powers connected devices and real\\-time systems—without the constraints of a traditional full\\-time role.
 



  





**What You’ll Do** 



* Design and develop
 **embedded software and system\\-level applications**
* Build software for microcontrollers, processors, and embedded devices
* Develop and maintain device drivers, middleware, and communication interfaces
* Work with RTOS, embedded Linux, and real\\-time systems where applicable
* Integrate sensors, peripherals, and hardware components
* Implement communication protocols such as UART, SPI, I2C, CAN, BLE, and MQTT
* Debug hardware\\-software interactions and optimize system performance
* Conduct testing, validation, and troubleshooting of embedded applications
* Collaborate with hardware, firmware, and product teams throughout development



  





**What We’re Looking For** 



* Strong experience in
 **embedded software development**
* Proficiency in C/C\\+\\+ and embedded programming concepts
* Experience with microcontrollers such as STM32, ESP32, ARM Cortex, or similar platforms
* Familiarity with RTOS, embedded Linux, or low\\-level system programming
* Understanding of communication protocols and hardware interfaces
* Experience with debugging tools, oscilloscopes, logic analyzers, and JTAG tools
* Knowledge of IoT systems and edge computing is a plus
* Strong problem\\-solving and troubleshooting skills
* A self\\-driven freelancer who can independently deliver embedded software solutions



  





 Why BeGig
 


* Flexible freelance opportunities aligned with your embedded systems expertise
* Work with multiple clients building next\\-generation hardware and IoT products
* Get matched with relevant, high\\-quality embedded engineering engagements through the BeGig platform
* Be part of a trusted network of hardware and software professionals



  





**Apply now** 
 to join BeGig as an Embedded Software Engineer and help power innovative connected devices and intelligent systems. 🚀

title: Lead Software Test Engineer
description:
Software Test Lead — SaaS / Mobile App
 
**Location:** 
 Hybrid / Kochi
 **Salary:** 
 ₹60,000 – ₹1,00,000 / month
 



**Reports to:** 
 CEO (Founder's Office)
 



**Team:** 
 Core Engineering — embedded across web, PWA, and mobile
 



  




 The Mission
 
 We ship fast. We ship across a SaaS web platform, progressive web apps, and React Native mobile apps — often all in the same sprint. Speed is our advantage, but speed without quality is just a faster way to lose trust.
 



 That's where you come in.
 



 This is the person who owns the question
 **"Is it actually good enough to ship?"** 
 Not as a gatekeeper who slows everyone down, but as the engineer who breaks things
 *before* 
 users do. You are not joining an existing QA machine to push tickets through it — you are building the QA discipline from the ground up and setting the bar the whole team is measured against.
 



 If you've ever watched a release go out and thought
 *"I would have caught that,"* 
 this role is for you.
 



  




 Your Role
 
 You own quality end\\-to\\-end across the product portfolio. You define how we test, you build the systems that let us test at speed, and you hold the line on what ships.
 


* **Own the Test Strategy:** 
 Define the testing approach for each product surface — web app, PWA, and mobile. You decide what gets automated, what stays manual, and what the regression coverage actually needs to be. This is yours to architect, not inherit.
* **Automate Ruthlessly:** 
 Build and maintain automated test suites (E2E, integration, regression) so the team isn't re\\-testing the same flows by hand every release. Think Playwright / Cypress for web, Appium / Detox for mobile — whatever fits the stack and scales.
* **API \\& Integration Testing:** 
 Our products live and die on APIs and third\\-party integrations (payments, messaging, auth). You test contracts, edge cases, and failure modes — not just the happy path. REST and GraphQL should be second nature.
* **Mobile \\& Cross\\-Platform:** 
 Test across real devices, OS versions, and screen sizes. Catch the icon that doesn't render, the deep link that breaks, the crash on sign\\-out before it hits a user's phone.
* **Quality Gates in CI/CD:** 
 Embed automated checks into the deployment pipeline so quality is enforced by the system, not by someone remembering to test. A broken build should never reach staging quietly.
* **Bug Triage \\& Release Sign\\-off:** 
 You run the triage. You write clear, reproducible bug reports developers don't have to chase you about. And when it's time to ship, your sign\\-off means something.
* **Mentor \\& Set the Standard:** 
 As we grow the QA function, you'll bring in and level up others. The testing culture of this company will look like whatever you build it to look like.



  




 Who You Are
 * **4–7 years in QA / SDET** 
 , with real exposure to owning or leading a testing effort — not just executing someone else's test plan.
* **Automation\\-first by instinct.** 
 You'd rather spend two days writing a suite than two weeks clicking through the same flows. You've shipped automated test frameworks that other people actually relied on.
* **Comfortable across web
 *and* 
 mobile.** 
 You've tested SaaS web apps, and you've tested native or cross\\-platform mobile (React Native / Flutter / native). You know the failure modes are different and you treat them differently.
* **API\\-literate.** 
 You can read an API spec, write tests against it, and reason about what happens when a downstream service times out or returns garbage.
* **A startup operator.** 
 You're comfortable with ambiguity, missing documentation, and shipping in motion. You build the process the company needs, not the process your last job had.
* **A clear communicator.** 
 Your bug reports are reproducible. Your sign\\-offs are trusted. Engineers don't dread your comments — they rely on them.



  




 Non\\-Negotiable Requirement
 
**AI\\-Native Workflow.** 
 This isn't a nice\\-to\\-have — it's how we work. You're expected to use AI to multiply your testing output: generating and maintaining test cases, drafting automation scripts, and standing up
 **agentic test flows** 
 that explore the product the way a user would, at a scale no manual tester could match. We're building toward QA that runs continuously, not just before release. You should be excited to lead that, not learning it on the job.
 



 Proficiency with AI\\-assisted environments (Cursor, Claude, or equivalent) for writing and maintaining test code is assumed.
 



  




 Bonus Points
 * Hands\\-on familiarity with our stack:
 **Django / Python** 
 backends,
 **React** 
 front\\-ends,
 **React Native (Expo)** 
 mobile,
 **GCP (Cloud Run)** 
 , and
 **Supabase** 
 .
* Experience testing
 **PWAs** 
 and offline\\-capable apps.
* Performance and security testing chops.
* Experience integrating test automation into GCP / AWS / cloud\\-native CI/CD.

title: Engineering Team Lead
description:
**Job Description** 




  





 You will be the senior engineer and technical leader for our AI\\-powered branding platform.
 



 You will architect, build, and build the AI platform that empowers IMERGEY's customers. You will work hands\\-on daily, writing code, reviewing strategy, setting up infrastructure, and making key technical decisions, while leading and mentoring 2 junior developers.
 



  





 We are not looking for a manager who delegates. We need someone who has built a platform from scratch before, knows the right tools for AI integration, content scheduling, analytics, and human\\-in\\-the\\-loop workflows, and isn’t afraid to debug at 10 PM before a launch.
 



  





**Job Highlights** 



* End\\-to\\-end Digital Branding Responsibilities as a Copywriter
* Client \\& Stakeholder Management
* Content Research and Writing
* Provide strategic social media advice
* Degree holder / Student in Marketing, Digital Marketing, Journalism, Communications or related disciplines



  





**Core Responsibilities** 



* Own the end\\-to\\-end technical stack: frontend, backend, database, AI integrations, and DevOps
* Build the MVP (months 1–3\\) and iterate based on user feedback
* Integrate AI APIs (OpenAI GPT\\-4, Anthropic Claude, or open\\-source models for post generation, sentiment analysis, brand persona creation)
* Implement content scheduling \\& publishing to LinkedIn, Twitter/X, Medium (REST APIs or Zapier\\-like connectors)
* Build analytics engine: engagement prediction, sentiment tracking, brand health scoring
* Create the “human touch” workflow (ticketing, assignment to brand strategists, feedback loops)



  





**Team Leadership \\& Mentorship (30% by month 6\\)** 



* Manage 2 junior developers (full\\-stack or frontend/backend split)
* Conduct daily standups, code reviews, pair programming sessions
* Define coding standards, Git workflow, testing strategy
* Delegate well\\-scoped tasks (UI components, API integrations, bug fixes) while you focus on complex systems (AI orchestration, scaling, compliance \\& security)



  





**Technical Strategy \\& Roadmap** 



* Translate product roadmap (1\\-year milestones) into technical sprints
* Choose and justify technology stack (see below)
* Plan for scalability: from 100 to 10,000 users (database indexing, caching, async jobs)
* Ensure data privacy \\& security (user content, API keys, PII compliance)
* Manage allocated budgets



  





**Hand\\-on Problem Solving** 



* Debug production issues across frontend, backend, and third\\-party APIs
* Optimize AI prompt latency and token costs
* Build internal tools for the sales \\& marketing team (lead scoring, demo tracking)
* Automate deployment (CI/CD) and monitoring (error tracking, uptime)



  





**Product Collaboration** 



* Work directly with sr. management and head of marketing to scope features
* Estimate development effort for quarterly milestones
* Participate in user interviews to validate technical assumptions



  





**Requirements** 



* 5\\+ years of software engineering experience, with at least 2 years in a tech lead or founding engineer role
* Has built at least one production SaaS platform from scratch (MVP to 1,000\\+ users)
* Deep experience with AI/LLM integrations (OpenAI, LangChain, prompt engineering)
* Strong proficiency in one modern stack (Node.js \\+ React OR Python \\+ Next.js)
* Experience with social media APIs (LinkedIn, Twitter, or Meta)
* Comfortable with DevOps: CI/CD, cloud hosting (AWS/GCP), environment management
* Can write clean, tested, documented code and enforce quality with juniors
* It would be nice to have if you have built a Built a content scheduling or automation tool before
* Player\\-coach mentality – you’d rather code than attend meetings, but you love teaching



  





  





**Why Join Us?** 




  




* Build from zero – your architecture decisions will define the company
* High ownership – you’re not a cog; this is a high impact role with high responsibility
* Meaningful effort – we want you to have life\\-changing upside
* Work with AI – LLMs, fine\\-tuning, agents (not just CRUD apps)
* Real human impact – helping creators and professionals grow their brands
* Remote\\-first, flexible – no pointless meetings

title: Embedded Firmware Senior Developer
description:
**Position Title** 
 : Embedded Firmware – Senior Developer
 



**Position Type** 
 : Full\\-time
 



**Duration** 
 : Long Term
 



**Work Location** 
 : Pune, MH
 



**Work Preference** 
 : Onsite
 



  





**Job Description** 




 We’re looking for an experienced
 **Senior Firmware Design Engineer** 
 with
 **7\\-10 years** 
 of experience to design, develop, and validate embedded software and test applications for automated test equipment used in manufacturing environments for fire alarm panels, control boards, and related assemblies.
 



 This role focuses on development of reliable, maintainable, and production\\-ready firmware/software that automates fixture operation, controls instruments, programs DUT firmware, executes test sequences, logs traceable results, and reduces operator dependency through robust workflows and error\\-proof user interaction. The engineer will own software design and implementation for fixture control, DUT communication, sequencing, diagnostics, and manufacturing integration.
 



 \\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_
 



**Key Responsibilities** 




**Test Software \\& Fixture Control Development** 




 • Design and implement software or embedded firmware for automated functional test systems, fixture controllers, and production validation workflows.
 



 • Develop modular test frameworks, sequencing logic, and reusable software components for DUT power\\-up, programming, communication, measurement, decision\\-making, and reporting.
 



 • Implement deterministic and robust control of fixture IO, interlocks, indicators, relays, sensors, and operator workflow states.
 



 • Support embedded controller firmware and PC\\-connected test application development as required by the selected architecture.
 



**DUT Communication, Programming \\& Diagnostics** 




 • Develop communication layers for interaction with DUTs over interfaces such as UART, I2C, SPI, CAN, Ethernet, USB, RS485, or other product\\-specific protocols as applicable.
 



 • Implement firmware download / programming flows, version checks, device configuration, and post\\-program functional verification.
 



 • Develop diagnostic hooks, register access utilities, and production test modes to improve observability and fault isolation.
 



 • Coordinate with product firmware teams where special test firmware or diagnostic modes are needed.
 



**Automated Test Execution \\& Result Logic** 




 • Implement automated execution of electrical, logical, timing, interface, and user\\-facing functional tests based on defined requirements and pass/fail criteria.
 



 • Build retries, timeout, fault\\-handling, and safe recovery logic to ensure robust test execution in production.
 



 • Develop pass/fail classification logic, detailed error code generation, and failure reporting to support clear segregation of good and bad units.
 



 • Support data logging, traceability, serial\\-number association, and integration with manufacturing applications.
 



**Operator UI, Error\\-Proofing \\& Serviceability** 




 • Develop production\\-friendly operator interfaces that are simple, guided, and difficult to misuse.
 



 • Implement step\\-by\\-step workflows, fixture status indication, prompts, alarms, error messaging, and clear troubleshooting guidance for low\\-skill operators.
 



 • Support features such as scanned serial number association, result traceability, fail logging, remote troubleshooting support, and field upgradability.
 



 • Reduce operator dependence through software\\-controlled sequencing, automated transitions, and built\\-in validation checks.
 



**Validation, Debugging \\& Production Support** 




 • Debug communication, timing, programming, sequencing, and instrument\\-control issues during bring\\-up and pilot\\-line deployment.
 



 • Use logs, protocol analysers, oscilloscopes, logic analysers, and diagnostic data to investigate false failures, intermittent issues, and test escapes.
 



 • Validate repeatability, throughput targets, changeover behavior, fault detection, and known\\-good / known\\-bad discrimination.
 



 • Support manufacturing rollout, early production issues, and continuous improvement of test coverage and stability.
 



**Documentation \\& Release** 




 • Create software design documentation, test sequence documentation, source\\-code documentation, revision history, and release packages.
 



 • Support validation reports, operator work instructions, troubleshooting guides, and handoff documentation.
 



 • Maintain source code and configuration control for production releases and future upgrades.
 



 \\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_
 



**Qualifications** 




 • Experience: 7\\-10 years in embedded firmware, test software, industrial automation software, or manufacturing test development.
 



 • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Electronics, Electrical Engineering, Instrumentation, or related discipline.
 



 • Strong expertise in Embedded C/C\\+\\+ and Python, with hands\\-on experience in hardware interfacing, automation logic, and debug.
 



 • Experience with communication protocols such as UART, I2C, SPI, CAN, RS485/RS232, Ethernet, or similar device\\-control interfaces.
 



 • Hands\\-on experience with test sequencing, instrument communication, logging, diagnostics, and production support.
 



 • Experience with DUT programming, manufacturing test systems, or hardware validation workflows preferred.
 



 • Strong problem\\-solving, debugging, and system\\-level thinking skills.
 



 • Ability to collaborate effectively with hardware, mechanical, manufacturing, and quality teams.
 



 \\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_\\_
 



**Tech Stack \\& Tools** 




 • Programming: Embedded C/C\\+\\+, Python
 



 • Platforms: Microcontrollers or industrial embedded controllers as required by fixture architecture
 



 • Interfaces \\& Protocols: UART, I2C, SPI, CAN, RS485/RS232, Ethernet, USB, GPIO control
 



 • Development Tools: IAR, vendor IDEs, Python\\-based automation environments, protocol debug tools
 



 • Version Control: Git/GitHub
 



 • Debug Tools: Oscilloscope, logic analyser, protocol analysers, serial debug tools
 



 • Production Software Features: Data logging, traceability, report generation, serial\\-number association, firmware programming support, remote diagnostics support

 Job Description:
   

  

 We are seeking a highly skilled Solidworks Electrical Application Engineer to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in electrical engineering and experience with Solidworks Electrical software. In this role, you will be responsible for providing technical support, developing electrical designs, and collaborating with cross\\-functional teams to ensure the successful implementation of electrical systems.

 We offer a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits package. If you are a motivated and detail\\-oriented professional looking to advance your career in electrical engineering, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity.
